publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-03-31 00:03:43 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Ithaca Lesbian and Gay Task Force records, 1984-1990, including mailing lists, correspondence and internal memos, flyers for events, a summary of accomplishments, 1984-1986, information on the Ithaca Fair Practices Ordinance passed in September 1984 (Chapter 29 of the Ithaca Municipal Code) making it illegal to discriminate on the basis of sexual orientation, suggestions for the platform of the Democratic Party of Tompkins County, minutes, Miller's own meeting notes, copies of OUTLINES, the Task Force's newsletter, and grant proposals, notes, and flyers for the 1989 Ithaca Lesbian and Gay Arts Festival. Also, other gay-related materials collected by Miller, 1985-1990, including Cornell University Gay PAC material, gay political material from Pittsburgh in 1986, and newsletters of NOGLSTP (National Organization of Gay and Lesbian Scientists and Technical Professionals)

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Andrea HektorPeter Martinez, June 2003Ezra Corral, July 2008

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Michael Miller attended Cornell University as a graduate student and held a post-doctoral position. He was actively involved in the Ithaca Lesbian and Gay Task Force and in Cornell's Gay PAC, a lesbian and gay student organization.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Ithaca Lesbian and Gay Task Force records, 1984-1990, including mailing lists, correspondence and internal memos, flyers for events, a summary of accomplishments, 1984-1986, information on the Ithaca Fair Practices Ordinance passed in September 1984 (Chapter 29 of the Ithaca Municipal Code) making it illegal to discriminate on the basis of sexual orientation, suggestions for the platform of the Democratic Party of Tompkins County, minutes, Miller's own meeting notes, copies of OUTLINES, the Task Force's newsletter, and grant proposals, notes, and flyers for the 1989 Ithaca Lesbian and Gay Arts Festival. Also, other gay-related materials collected by Miller, 1985-1990, including Cornell University Gay PAC material, gay political material from Pittsburgh in 1986, and newsletters of NOGLSTP (National Organization of Gay and Lesbian Scientists and Technical Professionals)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Michael Miller papers, #7436. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Gay liberation movement -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
controlaccess.corpname: Ithaca Lesbian and Gay Task Force

did.unittitle: Ithaca Lesbian and Gay Task Force (ILGTF) Correspondence, clippings, fliers, and other miscellaneous printed material
Miscellaneous printed material, including newsletters, broadsides, etc., particularly National Organization of Gay and Lesbian Scientists and Technical Professionals
